expectations.text: Uses preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution (soft) podAffinity, not requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution (hard), and explains the first-deploy deadlock risk of the hard variant
expectations.evidence: Uses `preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution` with explanation: "Hard self-referential affinity deadlocks — no node has an app=my-app pod yet on first deploy, so all pods stay Pending forever."

expectations.text: Specifies topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname in the podAffinityTerm to co-locate Pods on the same node
expectations.evidence: Shows `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname` in the podAffinityTerm.

expectations.text: Sets accessModes: ReadWriteOnce on the PVC because Hetzner Cloud Volumes do not support RWX
expectations.evidence: Shows `accessModes: - ReadWriteOnce` on the PVC with storageClassName: hcloud-volumes.

expectations.text: Mentions Longhorn as the alternative that enables RWX and allows Pods to spread across nodes without podAffinity
expectations.evidence: "If you need pods spread across nodes (for fault tolerance), switch to Longhorn which supports RWX. Then drop the podAffinity entirely."

expectations.text: Avoids recommending ReadWriteMany on Hetzner Cloud Volumes, which does not support that access mode
expectations.evidence: Uses ReadWriteOnce with podAffinity for Hetzner; RWX only mentioned for Longhorn alternative.

expectations.text: Mentions Longhorn as the alternative that enables RWX and allows Pods to spread across nodes without podAffinity

expectations.text: Avoids recommending ReadWriteMany on Hetzner Cloud Volumes, which does not support that access mode
expectations.evidence: Recommends NFS with ReadWriteMany as Option 1, and Longhorn with ReadWriteMany as Option 2. Does not use Hetzner with RWO + podAffinity.

expectations.text: Recommends StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates, not Deployment + single shared PVC, because each DB replica needs independent state
expectations.evidence: Uses StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates. "Why NOT a single PVC with a Deployment: A shared volume means all replicas write to the same directory — PostgreSQL requires exclusive access."

expectations.text: States that volumeClaimTemplates auto-names PVCs as <template-name>-<statefulset-name>-<ordinal> (e.g., data-cache-0)
expectations.evidence: "This creates PVCs named data-postgres-0, data-postgres-1, data-postgres-2 — one per replica."

expectations.text: Explicitly warns that deleting the StatefulSet does NOT delete the PVCs and they must be deleted by name
expectations.evidence: "kubectl delete statefulset postgres does NOT delete the PVCs. Delete them explicitly after verification: kubectl delete pvc data-postgres-0 data-postgres-1 data-postgres-2 -n db"

expectations.text: Warns against kubectl delete pvc -l <label> because label-based bulk delete can hit unintended PVCs in the namespace
expectations.evidence: Shows explicit deletion by name but does not warn against label-based bulk deletion with kubectl delete pvc -l.

expectations.text: Notes that no podAffinity is needed because each Pod has its own PVC and can schedule on any node
expectations.evidence: "Pods can schedule on different nodes (no affinity needed since each has its own RWO volume)."

expectations.text: Recommends StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates, not Deployment + single shared PVC, because each DB replica needs independent state
expectations.evidence: Uses StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates. "Why NOT a Deployment with a single PVC: A Deployment shares one PVC across all replicas — all 3 Postgres instances would write to the same volume. This causes data corruption."

expectations.text: States that volumeClaimTemplates auto-names PVCs as <template-name>-<statefulset-name>-<ordinal> (e.g., data-cache-0)
expectations.evidence: "Creates one PVC per replica: postgres-data-postgres-0, postgres-data-postgres-1, postgres-data-postgres-2."

expectations.evidence: "PVCs persist even if the StatefulSet is deleted (you must delete them manually)."

expectations.evidence: No warning against label-based bulk PVC deletion.

expectations.evidence: Shows volume mount with `readOnly: true`. "Volume mounts are preferred over envFrom because env vars are visible in crash dumps, /proc, and docker inspect."

expectations.text: Explains that envFrom env vars never update at runtime — they are set at container start — so a rollout restart is always required
